response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack/web engineer with nearly two decades of software experience, but the role requires deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. The closest matches are low-latency DLang systems work at Dunnhumby and brief C/C++ tooling work at National Instruments, which fall well short of principal-level performance architecture for deep-learning workloads.
strengths: Extensive engineering experience (15+ years) meeting the BS + 8 years experience bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and technical teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience
No deep neural network training/inference optimization or GPU workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture or hardware-software co-design

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI accelerator or DNN workload optimization background
Solid systems-level and low-latency software experience
